findings.title: Runtime backend public-skill list omits `$close` despite defining it as the pause/clear UX
findings.rationale: The runtime backend doc declares a canonical public skill list that excludes `$close`, but the same document later states `$close` is the public lease pause/clear path and a dedicated public `$close` skill exists. This creates a contradictory contract in the surface docs and can misroute operators away from the intended first-class pause UX.
findings.suggested_next_action: Update the top-level public skill list (and responsibilities section if needed) to include `$close`, then add/extend a regression assertion so the list cannot drift away from the documented pause/clear contract.
